title: Core Concepts in Ancient History
body:
The central concepts in ancient history include civilisation, empire, city-state, kingship, law code, temple economy, trade network, and collapse. These concepts help explain how societies organised power and meaning.

Because evidence is incomplete, historians also care about material culture, chronology, inscription, archaeology, and comparative inference. A single coin or shard of pottery can shift a whole interpretation.

The best ancient history is comparative. It asks not only what happened in Greece or Rome, but what similar or different things happened in Egypt, Persia, China, India, or the Americas.

That comparative lens prevents the field from collapsing into one civilisation’s self-importance.
furtherReading: The Silk Roads by Peter Frankopan
A History of the Ancient World by Susan Wise Bauer
